sections.label: Backend Service Architecture

annotations.text: Zone containers encode trust boundaries — client, edge, and internal zones visually separated
Auth service is the convergence point every request must cross

decisions: Zone rectangles encode trust boundaries rather than free-floating nodes
Data stores in bottom zone reinforces read/write dependency direction

sections.label: Registration Sequence

annotations.text: API Gateway is the single synchronous choke point for all three phases
Redis appears in two roles — session store (signup) and cache layer (dashboard)

decisions: Mermaid sequence used because content is time-ordered actor choreography
Note annotations preserved as visual phase dividers

sections.label: Onboarding State Machine

annotations.text: LR direction shows temporal progression — left is earlier, right is completion
Failure exits (Suspended, Abandoned) visually below the happy path

decisions: Red dashed edges for suspension, dotted gray for abandonment
Abandoned uses dashed border to signal recoverable vs terminal

sections.label: Data Model (ER)

annotations.text: users is the hub — all 5 satellite tables have FK to users.id
avatars is one-to-one; all others are one-to-many

decisions: 6 tables covering all onboarding data needs: users, sessions, onboarding_steps, activity_log, email_queue, avatars

sections.label: Error & Recovery Flows

annotations.text: 9 failure modes in 4 colored zones — every error has a recovery path
Diamond = failure trigger, Rectangle = recovery strategy

decisions: Zones colored by domain: orange=entry, red=auth, yellow=upload, purple=data
Circuit breaker and graceful degradation for infrastructure-level recoveries

sections.label: Security Boundaries

annotations.text: 5 trust zones top-to-bottom: threats enter from top, filtered downward
JWT lifecycle isolated in its own boundary — token mgmt is the single access control chokepoint

decisions: TB direction makes zone boundaries read as physical security perimeter stack
Auth boundary sits between edge and app — no request reaches app without JWT verification
bcrypt and session manager in auth boundary, not app zone

findings: 6 views cover all system facets: topology, runtime flow, lifecycle, data shape, failure modes, trust boundaries
JWT is the single chokepoint — every request passes through it
Redis serves 3 roles: session store, rate counter, dashboard cache
9 error modes mapped with corresponding recovery strategies
5 trust zones from untrusted internet to encrypted data layer
